searchDocs.title: USE_CLIENT_SERVER​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Settings.USE_CLIENT_SERVER: boolean Set this to false if you don't want to use client-server functionality (false = fully server side destructions).

searchDocs.title: DefaultGridSize​
searchDocs.content: PartDivision </> Settings.DefaultGridSize: number The default GridSize to use if none is provided through a GridSize number attribute. TIP It is reccomended to set the GridSize property on parts according to their size so that your performance is the most consistent. Larger parts should have larger GridSize, especially when using Greedy Meshing.

searchDocs.title: UseGreedyMeshing​
searchDocs.content: PartDivision </> Settings.UseGreedyMeshing: boolean Whether or not to use Greedy Meshing. NOTE Keep in mind that Greedy Meshing is an O(n^3) algorithm, so designing your map for voxel destruction is critical.

searchDocs.title: NonDivisibleInteraction​
searchDocs.content: PartDivision </> Settings.NonDivisibleInteraction: "FALL" | "DESTROY" | "NONE" What happens to destructible parts that cannot be divided by Shatterbox.

searchDocs.title: UseSmoothCleanup​
searchDocs.content: SmoothCleanup </> Settings.UseSmoothCleanup: boolean If false, the CleanupDelay will be ignored and no smooth cleanups will happen at all.

searchDocs.title: DefaultSmoothCleanupDelay​
searchDocs.content: SmoothCleanup </> Settings.DefaultSmoothCleanupDelay: number The default smooth cleanup delay if none is provided. TIP Setting the CleanupDelay to 0 or a negative number disables smooth cleanup for that destruction. So you can set this to 0 to make smooth cleanups opt-in per destruction.

searchDocs.title: PuppetMaxCount​
searchDocs.content: Puppeteer </> Settings.PuppetMaxCount: number The maximum number of puppets allowed in existence before old ones start getting replaced by new ones. DANGER This setting has a hard cap of 8191.

searchDocs.title: PuppetSleepVelocity​
searchDocs.content: Puppeteer </> Settings.PuppetSleepVelocity: number How slow a puppet has to be moving to not get replicated (and eventually become anchored)

searchDocs.title: PuppetAnchorTimeout​
searchDocs.content: Puppeteer </> Settings.PuppetAnchorTimeout: number How long a puppet moving slower than PuppetSleepVelocity has before becoming anchored (seconds)

searchDocs.title: PuppetReplicationFrequency​
searchDocs.content: Puppeteer </> Settings.PuppetReplicationFrequency: number How frequently puppet CFrames are sent to each client (default 20 fps target replication frequency) TIP This setting effectively limits the maximum Recv of Puppets. You can consider this setting to be the target in kilobytes/second (20KB/s max by default)

searchDocs.title: ClientTweenPuppets​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Puppeteer </> Settings.ClientTweenPuppets: boolean If true, the client will tween between puppet CFrame replications (Client-side only setting)

searchDocs.title: ClientTweenDistanceLimit​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the client. Client Puppeteer </> Settings.ClientTweenDistanceLimit: number Voxels farther than this many studs away don't get interpolated (Client-side only setting)

searchDocs.title: SkipInstanceCheck​
searchDocs.content: PartDivision </> Settings.SkipInstanceCheck(i: Instance ) → boolean A function that you can use to skip instances with code. Return true to skip the instance "i".

searchDocs.title: DestructDelay​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.DestructDelay: number? The delay in seconds between destructions when using Hitbox:Start

searchDocs.title: DestructionType​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.DestructionType: "DEFAULT" | "IMAGINARY" Determines what kind of destruction is performed while using Hitbox:Start. If set to "IMAGINARY", then Hitbox.ImaginaryCallback is used to give you the imaginary voxels and existing debris.

searchDocs.title: ImaginaryCallback​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.ImaginaryCallback: (( imaginaryVoxels: {ImaginaryVoxel}, existingDebris: {BasePart } ) → ())? A callback that runs if Hitbox.DestructionType is set to "IMAGINARY" while using Hitbox:Start. It supplies you the imaginary voxels and any existing debris.

searchDocs.title: StartConnectionEvent​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.StartConnectionEvent: RBXScriptSignal  Polling event for Hitbox:Start.

searchDocs.title: WeldConnectionEvent​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.WeldConnectionEvent: RBXScriptSignal  Polling event for Hitbox:WeldTo.

searchDocs.title: VelocityPrediction​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.VelocityPrediction: boolean Whether or not to predict the velocity of the part the hitbox is welded to. Only works if the hitbox is welded to a BasePart using Hitbox:WeldTo.

searchDocs.title: VelocityBias​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox.VelocityBias: number How many frames ahead to predict when using Hitbox.VelocityPrediction.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:Destroy() → () Equivalent to Shatterbox.Destroy(Hitbox). Clears Hitbox.ExcludePlayersReplication.

searchDocs.title: ImaginaryVoxels​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:ImaginaryVoxels() → ( {ImaginaryVoxel}, {BasePart } ) Equivalent to Shatterbox.ImaginaryVoxels(Hitbox). Clears Hitbox.ExcludePlayersReplication.

searchDocs.title: WeldTo​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:WeldTo(Part: BasePart ) → () Will "weld" the hitbox to the given part, setting Hitbox.CFrame to be equal to Part.CFrame whenever Hitbox.WeldConnectionEvent fires. If Part is destroyed using Instance:Destroy(), the hitbox will automatically call Hitbox:DestroyHitbox If workspace.PlayerCharacterDestroyBehavior is false, the hitbox will not be automatically stopped when that Player respawns if you have welded to their root part.

searchDocs.title: Unweld​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:Unweld() → () Will "unweld" the hitbox if it has been welded after using Hitbox:WeldTo.

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:Start() → () Will start to cut around the hitbox every Hitbox.DestructDelay seconds, which defaults to 0 (every frame). The hitbox updates every time the Hitbox.StartConnectionEvent fires, which is expected to supply a deltaTime parameter. If Hitbox.DestructionType is set to "IMAGINARY", then Hitbox.ImaginaryCallback is used to give you the imaginary voxels and existing debris.

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:Stop() → () Will stop cutting around the hitbox if the hitbox is active after using Hitbox:Start.

searchDocs.title: DestroyHitbox​
searchDocs.content: </> Hitbox:DestroyHitbox() → () Will call Hitbox:Unweld and then Hitbox:Stop. Calling each individually is equivalent to calling this function.

searchDocs.title: CuttingPart​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.CuttingPart: (ImaginaryPart | Part | Model )? The virtual/physical hitbox that the destruction will use. If this is not provided, CFrame, Size, and Shape, will be used. You must provide one or the other. If this is a Model, a destruction will be queued for every Part descendant of that Model

searchDocs.title: CFrame​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.CFrame: CFrame?

searchDocs.title: Size​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.Size: Vector3?

searchDocs.title: Shape​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.Shape: PartType?

searchDocs.title: FilterTagged​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.FilterTagged: (string | {string})? Only instances with the given tag(s) are considered for voxel destruction. If this is not passed, every BasePart without the "Indestructible" tag is considered.

searchDocs.title: CleanupDelay​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.CleanupDelay: number? The amount of time in seconds before this destruction is cleaned up. NOTE Set this to 0 to disable cleanups per-destruction NOTE New destructions can reset the cleanup timer of existing dirty groups, only if the new reset time would extend the time until cleanup. Example You destroy with a CleanupDelay of 10, wait 4 seconds, and then with a CleanupDelay of 2 in the same spot. You will have to wait 6 seconds for it to come back because 6 is greater than 2. If you instead waited 9 seconds, the time until cleanup would get extended to 2 seconds because 1 is less than 2.

searchDocs.title: SkipEncapsulatedVoxels​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.SkipEncapsulatedVoxels: boolean? This makes larger destructions an entire order of magnitude faster. Whether or not to intelligently skip voxels fully contained by the hitbox. If this is false, these fully contained parts are Voxelized entirely using math. Otherwise, they are directly deleted. NOTE If you use this parameter you will only recieve "edge" voxels.

searchDocs.title: SkipFloors​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.SkipFloors: boolean?

searchDocs.title: SkipWalls​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.SkipWalls: boolean?

searchDocs.title: OnVoxelDestruct​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.OnVoxelDestruct: string? The name of the OnVoxelDestruct effect you want to use for this destruction. You can register these effects either with Shatterbox.RegisterOnVoxelDestruct or by making an entry in the desired Shatterbox/effects module. Client side effects should be registered on the client side, while server side effects should be registered on the server side. Client side effects only work with Client-Server enabled. You should register any given effect only once in a static context, which you are then free to use anywhere else - even in other scripts. Shatterbox.RegisterOnVoxelDestruct("SomeName", function(voxel, info) voxel.Anchored = false return end) -- later or in another script hb.OnVoxelDestruct = "SomeName"

searchDocs.title: OnDestructCompleted​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.OnDestructCompleted: OnDestructCompleted? A callback that runs when the destruction is finished processing WARNING This only works with Shatterbox.Destroy or hitbox:Destroy(). For ImaginaryVoxels, you can use the array of imaginary voxels to obtain the same information.

searchDocs.title: UserData​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.UserData: {any}? A table that is passed to the OnVoxelDestruct effect as info.UserData. You can put variables in this table that are to be used in your effect.

searchDocs.title: WaitForClientTimeout​
searchDocs.content: </> DestructionParameters.WaitForClientTimeout: number? If set, how many seconds the server will wait at max for each client to recieve their static map parts. This only works with Client-Server enabled. Use this if you see the ground flashing due to the size of your destruction. DANGER If you use it, be sure to keep this set to a low value like 0.1 or 0.2, as exploiters/laggy players can force every destruction to take this maximum amount of time.

searchDocs.title: ExcludePlayersReplication​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> DestructionParameters.ExcludePlayersReplication: (Player | {Player })? A player or a list of players that will not recieve the destruction. This only works with Client-Server enabled. Use this on the server side if you have a client-initiated destruction, so the originating player doesn't recieve the same destruction they started. Server: SomeEvent.OnServerEvent:Connect(function(plr, destructionCFrame) -- make sure you add sanity checks so you don't get hacked. hitbox.CFrame = destructionCFrame -- do not replicate the destruction back to "plr" hitbox.ExcludePlayersReplication = plr -- make sure the server side hitbox and the client side hitbox are identical when you do this. Otherwise the destructon won't be synchronized. hitbox:Destroy() end) Client: hitbox:Destroy() SomeEvent:FireServer(hitbox.CFrame)

searchDocs.title: GridSize​
searchDocs.content: deprecated in Release 7 </> This was deprecated in Release 7 This item is deprecated. Do not use it for new work. DestructionParameters.GridSize: number? What GridSize this hitbox will divide parts down to. This can cause issues with greedy meshing if you destroy a part using multiple grid sizes. You should instead give parts a GridSize number attribute so each Part only has one GridSize.

searchDocs.title: OnDestructCompleted​
searchDocs.content: </> type OnDestructCompleted = ( voxelCount: number, affectedIDs: {[string]: boolean} ) → () A function that defines behavior to happen after a destruction is finished processing. voxelCount A number that dictates how many voxels this destruction destroyed. This number is identical whether or not DestructionParameters.SkipEncapsulatedVoxels is true. affectedIDs An array that contains every DirtyGroupID encountered during this destruction. Use this to obtain information such as the materials this destruction encountered. hb.OnDestructCompleted = function(count, affectedIDs) local materials = {} for DirtyGroupID in pairs(affectedIDs) do local original = Shatterbox.GetOriginalPart(DirtyGroupID) materials[original.Material] = true end for material in pairs(materials) do -- do something once for every material the destruction encountered end end

searchDocs.title: OnVoxelDestructType​
searchDocs.content: </> type OnVoxelDestructType = ( voxel: Part , info: VoxelInfo ) → () A function that is run for every voxel the destruction encounters. see DestructionParameters.OnVoxelDestruct for a brief explanation on how to use it.

searchDocs.title: VoxelInfo​
searchDocs.content: </> interface VoxelInfo { DirtyGroupID: string CuttingPart: ImaginaryPart IsEdge: boolean IsAlreadyDebris: boolean UserData: {any} } A table that contains information about the destroyed voxel DirtyGroupID The ID of the dirty group this voxel is a part of. A dirty group is a group of parts that all originated from the same part. CuttingPart The state of the hitbox as it was when the destruction was queued. IsEdge Whether or not this voxel lies along the edge of the destruction. IsAlreadyDebris Whether or not this voxel was already debris before this destruction destroyed it again. UserData User supplied information to use in the OnVoxelDestruct effect. Equivalent to DestructionParameters.UserData.

searchDocs.title: ImaginaryPart​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ImaginaryPart { CFrame: CFrame Size: Vector3 Shape: PartType } A table that represents a physical object

searchDocs.title: ImaginaryVoxel​
searchDocs.content: </> interface ImaginaryVoxel { CFrame: CFrame Size: Vector3 Shape: PartType DirtyGroupID: string IsEdge: boolean UserData: {any} } Represents a voxel that might be created. These are to be instantiated using Shatterbox.InstantiateImaginaryVoxel See Shatterbox.VoxelInfo for an explanation on these properties.

searchDocs.title: Puppeteer​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Shatterbox.Puppeteer(voxel: Part ) → () Server side function. Begins controlled replication of voxel to the clients according to the Puppeteer settings. When the part stops moving, it will become anchored to allow for a larger number of ground debris while still having good motion. It is safe to un-anchor these parts again in your destruction effects to have them start replicating again, they will still anchor themselves when they stop moving.

searchDocs.title: IsPuppet​
searchDocs.content: This item only works when running on the server. Server </> Shatterbox.IsPuppet(voxel: Part ) → boolean Returns true if the passed in part has had Shatterbox.Puppeteer called on it before. On the client side, this will always be false because the client sided puppets are indestructible.

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.Destroy(params: DestructionParameters | Hitbox) → () Queues voxel destruction to be performed around the hitbox according to the params supplied.

searchDocs.title: ImaginaryVoxels​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.ImaginaryVoxels(params: DestructionParameters | Hitbox) → ( {ImaginaryVoxel}, {BasePart } ) Instantly performs voxel destruction around the hitbox according to the params supplied. Returns the imaginary voxels to be created as well as any existing debris encountered. These are to be instantiated using Shatterbox.InstantiateImaginaryVoxel. WARNING If you use SkipEncapsulatedVoxels, the #length of the imaginary voxels array will show up as larger than it really is. This is to give the same functionality as OnDestructCompleted voxelCount without the need for a third return value. Use ipairs or generalized iteration to iterate over this array.

searchDocs.title: InstantiateImaginaryVoxel​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.InstantiateImaginaryVoxel( ImaginaryVoxel: ImaginaryVoxel, doNotGiveDebrisTag: boolean? ) → Part  Returns a created voxel using the supplied ImaginaryVoxel. Will automatically parent the voxel to the original parent of the group. Will also automatically give the voxel the "ShatterboxDebris" tag unless you pass in doNotGiveDebrisTag. TIP You would want to pass in doNotGiveDebrisTag if you're going to use the static function Shatterbox.OnVoxelDestruct to use/extend an existing effect.

searchDocs.title: ResetArea​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.ResetArea(area: Part | ImaginaryPart) → () Resets an area. Currently only supports Box shaped areas. Does not revert the ownership of parts with Client-Server enabled.

searchDocs.title: Reset​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.Reset(doNotRevertOwnership: boolean?) → () Resets all modified parts and re-allocates system queues/maps. If doNotRevertOwnership is true with Client-Server enabled, the clients do not delete their owned map parts.

searchDocs.title: CreateHitbox​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.CreateHitbox() → Hitbox Creates a Hitbox object which can be used in place of the destruction functions, and has additional functionality. (like VoxBreaker moveable hitbox) If you use ExcludePlayersReplication, that will be cleared after every destruction automatically, for convenience.

searchDocs.title: GetOriginalPart​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.GetOriginalPart(DirtyGroupID: string) → BasePart  Returns the original part that is associated with this DirtyGroupID. This part is never destroyed, just has its parent set to nil.

searchDocs.title: VoxelDistanceVector​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.VoxelDistanceVector( voxel: Part | ImaginaryPart, point: Vector3 ) → Vector3  Takes a point and converts it to the voxel space distance relative to voxel. This is a vector which tells you how many voxels away point is on each local axis, as fractional quantities. It is possible for an axis to be less than one, but never negative. TIP See Shatterbox/effects/Server/MappingVoxelSpace for an example on how to use VoxelDistanceVector

searchDocs.title: VoxelCountVector​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.VoxelCountVector( voxel: Part | ImaginaryPart, boxSize: Vector3 ) → Vector3  Returns a vector that tells you how many voxels (relative to voxel) a box determined by boxSize has on each local axis, as fractional quantities. It is not possible for an axis to be less than one, because there is always at least one voxel on every axis. TIP See Shatterbox/effects/Server/MappingVoxelSpace for an example on how to use VoxelCountVector

searchDocs.title: RegisterOnVoxelDestruct​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.RegisterOnVoxelDestruct( name: string, callback: OnVoxelDestructType ) → () Will register the given callback to the given name. If you call this function on the server side, you are creating a server-side effect. Vice-versa for registering on the client side. Consider using Shatterbox/effects modules for effect definitions. WARNING Register any given effect only once, you are free to use it by name in scripts other than the one it is defined in.

searchDocs.title: OnVoxelDestruct​
searchDocs.content: </> Shatterbox.OnVoxelDestruct( name: string, voxel: Part , info: VoxelInfo ) → () A function to manually call a registered OnVoxelDestruct effect. TIP This can be used as a way to constructively build on top of any existing effects, saving you time. Example -- BumpyFloorBreakWalls but it makes every voxel black Shatterbox.RegisterOnVoxelDestruct("ExtendingAnEffect", function(voxel, info) voxel.Color = Color3.new() Shatterbox.OnVoxelDestruct("BumpyFloorBreakWalls", voxel, info) end)
